title: Booker and International Booker Overview
focus: Explains the difference between the Booker Prize and the International Booker Prize, including a list of Indian Booker winners.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 114]
4.7. INTERNATIONAL BOOKER PRIZE
Why in the News?
Banu Mushtaq wins International Booker Prize for Kannada short story collection 'Heart Lamp'.
About International Booker Prize
- Launched as Man Booker International Prize in 2005.
- An annual literary prize awarded for a single book that is Translated into English and Published in the UK or Ireland.
- Objective: To encourage more reading of quality fiction from non-English languages.
- Promotes global fiction among English-speaking readers.
- Genres: Novels and short story collections.
Deepa Bashti - Translator
Ist time to a collection of short stories & a Kannada language work

[Page 115]
The Booker Prizes
Award Details:
- Awarded for: Best work of sustained fiction of the year, written in English and published in the UK or Ireland
- Location: Somerset House, Strand, London, England
- Country: United Kingdom
- Presented by: Booker, McConnell Ltd (1969–2001); Man Group (2002–2019); Crankstart (2019 onwards)
- Reward(s): £50,000
- First awarded: 1969
Indian Winners of the Booker Prize:
- 1997: Arundhati Roy - The God of Small Things
- 2006: Kiran Desai - The Inheritance of Loss
- 2008: Aravind Adiga - The White Tiger
About Booker Prize:
- Founded in 1969, the Booker Prize is open to English-language novels from any country that have been published in Britain and Ireland.
- It aimed to stimulate the reading and discussion of contemporary fiction.
- Some Indian-origin authors who won the prize include VS Naipaul (1971), Salman Rushdie (1981), Arundhati Roy (1997), among others.
Note: Booker Prize is given for fiction books written in English only whereas International Booker Prize is awarded for books translated into English.
evidenceBullets: Banu Mushtaq wins International Booker Prize for Kannada short story collection 'Heart Lamp'.
- Launched as Man Booker International Prize in 2005.
